coredata.dc:title: Palladium nanoparticles functionalized graphene quantum dots with molecularly imprinted polymer for electrochemical analysis of citrinin
coredata.dc:creator: Akyıldırım, Onur
Kardaş, Faruk
Beytur, Murat
Yüksek, Haydar
Atar, Necip
Yola, Mehmet Lütfi
coredata.dc:description: Citrinin (CIT) is mutagenic and resistant to decomposition. In addition, it is founded in many foods and causes the significant diseases in human body. In this report, an imprinted electrochemical surface based on glassy carbon electrode (GCE) modified with palladium nanoparticles (PdNPs) involved in 5-(4-Hydroxybenzylidenamino)-2-mercaptobenzimidazole (BZ) functionalized graphene quantum dots (GQDs) was formed for CIT analysis. The formation of the surfaces was characterized by scanning electron microscope (SEM), transmission electron microscope (TEM), electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS). CIT imprinted electrochemical surface was formed in the presence of 80.0mM pyrrole as monomer and 20.0mM CIT as template. The linearity range and the detection limit (LOD) of the developed nanosensorwere calculated as 1.0×10−9–5.0×10−9 M and 2.0×10−10 M, respectively.

Palladium nanoparticles
Graphene quantum dots
Characterization

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of BZ, GQDs and BZ/GQDs
Synthesis of Pd NPs and Pd NPs/BZ/GQDs nanocomposite
Preparation of CIT imprinted electrochemical surface and removal of CIT
Selectivity, stability, repeatability and reproducibility of MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM images of (A) bare GCE, (B) GQDs/GCE, (C) BZ/GQDs/GCE, (D) PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE and (E) MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE.
Cyclic voltammogram for the electropolymerization of pyrrole (80.0mM) containing CIT (20.0mM) in pH6.0 phosphate buffer solution.

(A) Fitting of impedance spectrum for 1.0mM [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− (1:1) in 0.1M KCl at bare GCE (curve a), GQDs/GCE (curve b), BZ/GQDs/GCE (curve c) and PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE (curve d); (B) EIS of (a) MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE (with template molecule); (b) MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE (removing template); (c) MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE (after rebinding of 5.0nM CIT) in 1.0mM [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− solution in 0.1M KCl.

(A) The differential pulse voltammograms of the electrodes in present work: (a) MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE in blank buffer solution, (b) NIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE after rebinding of 3.0nM CIT, (c) MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE after rebinding of 3.0nM CIT; (B) DPVs of different molecular imprinted electrodes after rebinding of 3.0nM CIT (a) bare GCE; (b) GQDs/GCE; (c) BZ/GQDs/GCE; (d) PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE.
The differential pulse voltammograms with different CIT concentrations in pH6.0 of PBS (from blank solution without CIT to 5.0×10−9 M).
The differential pulse voltammograms related to 5.0nM CIT, OCH A and OCH B.

head.ce:title: Palladium nanoparticles functionalized graphene quantum dots with molecularly imprinted polymer for electrochemical analysis of citrinin

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemical Engineering, Faculty of Engineering and Architecture, Kafkas University, Kars, Turkey

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Science Education, Faculty of Education, Erzincan University, Erzincan, Turkey

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science and Letters, Kafkas University, Kars, Turkey

ce:affiliation.ce:label: d
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Pamukkale University, Faculty of Engineering, Department of Chemical Engineering, Denizli, Turkey

ce:affiliation.ce:label: e
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: İskenderun Technical University, Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences, Department of Biomedical Engineering, Hatay, Turkey

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Citrinin (CIT) is mutagenic and resistant to decomposition. In addition, it is founded in many foods and causes the significant diseases in human body. In this report, an imprinted electrochemical surface based on glassy carbon electrode (GCE) modified with palladium nanoparticles (PdNPs) involved in 5-(4-Hydroxybenzylidenamino)-2-mercaptobenzimidazole (BZ) functionalized graphene quantum dots (GQDs) was formed for CIT analysis. The formation of the surfaces was characterized by scanning electron microscope (SEM), transmission electron microscope (TEM), electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS). CIT imprinted electrochemical surface was formed in the presence of 80.0mM pyrrole as monomer and 20.0mM CIT as template. The linearity range and the detection limit (LOD) of the developed nanosensorwere calculated as 1.0×10−9–5.0×10−9 M and 2.0×10−10 M, respectively.

ce:section.ce:para: CIT, 4,6-dihydro-8-hydroxyl-3,4,5-trimethyl-6-oxo-3H-2-benzopyran-7-carboxylic acid, is isolated from Penicilliumcitrinum. It is mutagenic and resistant to decomposition. Thus, it is in many foods (cheese and red yeast rice) [1,2]. Abundant CIT causes important diseases such as liver and kidney and has nephrotoxic and hepatotoxic effects [3,4]. Owing to these effects, CIT is one of the most dangerous chemicals in terms of World Health Organization. In the literature, high-performance liquid chromatography-fluorescence, [4] and micellar electrokinetic capillary chromatography (MEKC) [5] were presented as analytical methods for CIT detection. Nonetheless, these methods have troublesome steps for real sample analysis. In addition, there is much material consumption. Thus, the fast and sensitive analytical methods based on nanocomposite are urgent needed in the literature.

Especially, the significant developments are performed in the production of nanocomposite-supported materials for sensitive and selective nanosensors [6–13]. Nonetheless, there are negative catalytic performances in many sensor systems and electronic devices. To overcome this problem, carbon-based nanomaterials as graphene/graphene oxide (GO) are frequently used in terms of important electronic properties [14,15].

The molecular imprinting technique is based on polymerization around template. The specific cavities related to analyte molecule are easily formed in the polymerization process. Thanks to this method, we can create selective and sensitive sensor systems based on molecularly imprinting polymer (MIP) [16].

Up to now, there is no study on the electrochemical detection of ATR by palladium nanoparticles/molecular liquids/graphene quantum dots nanocomposite with molecularly imprinted polymer. Firstly, palladium nanoparticles/molecular liquids/graphene quantum dots nanocomposite was prepared and characterized by SEM, TEM, EIS and XPS. After that, MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE were developed in the presence of 80.0mM pyrrole and 20.0mM CIT. 1.0×10−9–5.0×10−9 M and 2.0×10−10 M were calculated for linearity range and LOD, respectively. In addition, the prepared electrode was applied to chicken egg samples for CIT detection with high selectivity and recovery in presence of ochratoxin A (OCH A) and ochratoxin B (OCH B). Finally, the present sensor was stable for 60days.

CIT, OCH A and OCH B were obtained from Sigma–Aldrich. The stock solutions of CIT (1.0mM) were prepared according to the literature [17]. Pyrrole, graphite powder, disodium tetrachloropalladate (Na2PdCl4), acetonitrile (MeCN), isopropyl alcohol (IPA), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), potassium permanganate (KMnO4) and N-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-N-ethylcarbodiimidehydrochloride (EDC) were purchased from Sigma–Aldrich (USA).

IviumStat (U·S) equipped with C3 cell stand was used for obtaining differential pulse voltammogram (DPV), cyclic voltammogram and electrochemical impedance curves. XPS and TEM characterizations were applied via PHI 5000 Versa Probe (F ULVAC-PHI, Inc., Japan/USA) and JEOL 2100 HRTEM, respectively. ZEISS EVO 50 analytic microscope (Germany) model was performed for SEM images.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of BZ, GQDs and BZ/GQDs
ce:section.ce:para: 5-Amino-2-mercaptobenzimidazole (0.01mol) was dissolved in acetic acid (20mL) and treated with4-hydroxybenzaldehyde (0.01mol). After that, evaporated at 50–55°C in vacuo. After several recrystallization, the compound BZ was formed (Scheme 1 ).GQDs was prepared according to our previous literature [18]. GQDs suspension was interacted with 0.2M EDC solution for 4h for activation of carboxylate groups. After that, activated GQDs was mixed with 1.0mM BZ at a 1:1 volume ratio for 2h (BZ/GQDs).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of Pd NPs and Pd NPs/BZ/GQDs nanocomposite
ce:section.ce:para: PdNPs were prepared according to our previous literature [19]. To prepare Pd NPs/BZ/GQDs nanocomposite, 1.0mgmL−1 of PdNPs solution was mixed with 0.1mgmL−1 of BZ/GQDs nanocomposite at volume ratio of 1:1. Finally, the Pd NPs/BZ/GQDs nanocomposite was stored at room temperature.
GCE was cleaned according to our previous report [20]. 1.0mg of Pd NPs/BZ/GQDs nanocomposite into 1.0mL of ethanol was dropped onto the glassy carbon surface and the modified electrode was dried.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of CIT imprinted electrochemical surface and removal of CIT
ce:section.ce:para: CIT imprinted electrochemical surface and the removal of CIT were performed according to our previous literature [17]. The MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE and the other electrodes in this article were stored in closed box without fluctuations of temperature and pressure. The reference and counter electrodes are Ag/AgCl(aq) and Pt wire, respectively.
The chicken egg samples were bought from supermarket (Turkey). The extraction and dilution procedure were achieved according to our previous report [16].

The presence of Pd NPs on BZ/GQDs nanocomposite (lighter structure) is seen clearly (Fig. 1 ). The average diameters of spherical Pd NPs as dark dots are 20–25nm. Hence, we can say that Pd NPs on BZ/GQDs nanocomposite was successfully prepared.

The unmodified glassy carbon surface has smooth surface (Fig. 2A) in investigations of SEM images. When the unmodified glassy carbon surface was modified with GQDs and BZ/GQDs, some layers were obtained (Fig. 2B and Fig. 2C). Fig. 2D shows an intensive layer relating to PdNPs incorporation. Fig. 2E indicates a layer of electrodeposition suggesting molecularly imprinting polymer.

In the XPS spectra (Fig. 3 ), C1s, N1s, S2p and Pd3d peaks of PdNPs/BZ/GQDs confirmed that the structure of BZ/GQDs was functionalized with PdNPs. The peaks of C1s at 284.3, 285.2, 286.2 and 287.5eV are related to CC/CC, CO, CO and OCO, respectively. In the N1s narrow region, the signal at 398.2eV is corresponded to CN groups. The peak at 400.8eV is attributed in unreacted BZ molecules. The signals of340.1 and 335.6eV indicated the presence of PdNPs on PdNPs/BZ/GQDs [18,19].
During the first scan, the oxidation potential of monomer was 0.92V (Fig. 4 ). The current signals at 0.92V diminished with subsequent scans. They vanished at 10th cycle on PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE.

According to Fig. 5A, the value of charge transfer resistance (Rct) of bare GCE was 195Ω (curve a of Fig. 5A). When the bare GCE was covered with GQDs, Rct value was obtained as 162Ω (curve b of Fig. 5A). This decrease results from superior conductivity and specific surface area of GQDs. When GQDs/GCE was covered with compound BZ (molecular liquid), the conductivity on electrode more increased (curve c of Fig. 5A). Finally, the addition of Pd NPs on nanocomposite enabled more catalytic activity (curve d of Fig. 5A). After pyrrole's polymerization on BZ/GQDs/GCE, Rct value was obtained as 130Ω (curve a of Fig. 5B). This situation indicated the obstruction effect of MIP film. After CIT removal on MIP/BZ/GQDs/GCE, the analyte molecule's recognition sites appeared again and Rct value decreased (curve b of Fig. 5B). When CIT's rebinding on electrode, the value of Rct increased to 80Ω (curve c of Fig. 5B).

The curve a of Fig. 6A shows the signal of MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE without template in 0.1M phosphate buffer solution (PBS) (pH6.0). As shown in Fig. 6A, there is no signal observation on the voltammogram (curve a). However, the prepared nanosensor demonstrates an obvious current signal at 0.92V (curve c of Fig. 6A). In addition, NIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE was prepared at the same conditions without template molecule. The small signal was observed in curve b of Fig. 6A. Finally, different molecular imprinted electrodes were prepared (Fig. 6B). According to Fig. 6B, the prepared MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE shows the most catalytic effect in comparison the other imprinted electrodes.

Fig. 7 indicated the relation between current signals and CIT concentrations. The regression equation is y (μA)=3.50×(nM)−0.1. The quantification limit (LOQ) and LOD for CIT were obtained as 1.0×10−9 M and 2.0×10−10 M, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity, stability, repeatability and reproducibility of MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE
ce:section.ce:para: The selectivity experiments were performed in the presence of OCH A and OCH B. According to Fig. 8 , the nanosensor for CIT in present work was 10.0 and 13.3 times selective in comparison with OCH A and OCH B. Hence, we can say that the nanosensor has good selectivity in present work.

According to recovery experiments (Table 1 ), owing to close values to 100.00%, the ingredients in chicken egg samples had no interference effect in analysis procedure. The technique of standard addition was applied to the chicken egg samples. The equation of standard addition is y (μA)=3.48×(nM)−1.19. Consequently, the matrix presence in chicken egg cannot importantly affect the selective analysis of CIT.

The one sensor was prepared for the stability of MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE. After that, the current signals were measured for 60days. The mean value of current signals is 97.01% of the first current signal. Thus, the nanosensor in present work is used in long-term for real sample analysis.

For the repeatability of MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE, the 15 differential pulse voltammograms were obtained in the presence of 5.0nM CIT. According to results, MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE had the repeated signals at about 16.8μA.

For reproducibility of MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs/GCE, 15 different MIP/PdNPs/BZ/GQDs modified electrodes were prepared via the same procedure. 0.82% of relative standard deviation for peak signal at 0.90V was observed. Hence, the reliability of the sensor preparation procedure is very good.

In this report, a new electrochemical sensor was developed and applied for CIT detection in chicken egg samples. According to the obtained data, the prepared PdNPs/BZ/GQDs was well characterized by using TEM, SEM, EIS and XPS. In present report, the cheaper, eco-friendly and highly efficient nanomaterial was obtained. The linearity range and LOD were obtained as1.0×10−9–5.0×10−9 M and 2.0×10−10 M, respectively. The sensor shows high selectivity, repeatability and reproducibility and sensitivity towards CIT detection in chicken egg matrix. The present sensor is stable for 60days. It also has high conductivity and catalytic effect. Because of these reasons, we can say that the electrochemical imprinted sensor was utilized for the routine analysis without interference.
